extra.message: User-controlled `username` query parameter is concatenated directly into a Jinja2 template string via `from_string()` on line 14. This allows Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI), enabling remote code execution. An attacker can supply e.g. `?username={{config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read()}}` to execute arbitrary commands on the server.

The SSTI-rendered output containing user-controlled data is returned via `HTMLResponse(content=output)` on line 19 without sanitization. Even if SSTI is prevented, an attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript through the `username` parameter (e.g. `?username=<script>alert(1)</script>`) which is reflected unsanitized as text/html content.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)
